title: The Wig Master

jokes.text: Have you noticed that every place has that tip jar on the counter?
jokes.setup: Opening observational premise about ubiquitous tip jars
jokes.punchline: The observation itself - the universality of tip jars
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational setup - economical and character-appropriate but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: What is the service that this tip is for, anyway? I mean, isn't the man basically just turning around?
jokes.setup: Questioning the nature of service deserving tips
jokes.punchline: Reducing service to just 'turning around'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reductionist observation - taking complex service interaction and reducing to physical movement

jokes.text: I think we're tipping people now just for the absence of outright hostility
jokes.setup: Building on the tip jar premise
jokes.punchline: Reframing tips as payment for not being attacked
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that reframes social courtesy as hostage situation - very quotable

jokes.text: Thanks very much, and here's something extra... for not taking my head... and smashing my face through the glass countertop. Really good service here.
jokes.setup: Previous joke about tipping for non-hostility
jokes.punchline: Extreme violent imagery delivered in polite thank-you format
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with darkly absurd imagery contrasted against polite service language

jokes.text: Are you out of your mind? You'd suffocate in there. You're lucky to have doors. When I was in the Army...
jokes.setup: George suggesting extending bathroom stall doors
jokes.punchline: Steinbrenner's extreme reaction about suffocation and military reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steinbrenner character moment - bombastic overreaction to mundane suggestion

jokes.text: Big Stein wants a little taste. Come on. Pass it down. That's a good boy.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner seeing George's calzone
jokes.punchline: Treating employee like a dog with 'good boy' while demanding food
jokes.explanation: Classic Steinbrenner treating employees like pets while referring to himself in third person

jokes.text: Big Stein wants an eggplant calzone. He must have one. Everybody out. Hurry up, hurry up.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner enjoying the calzone
jokes.punchline: Clearing entire meeting for calzone obsession
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Steinbrenner's impulsive nature prioritizing food over business

jokes.text: He bet me that Dustin Hoffman was in Star Wars.
jokes.setup: Explaining a bet she won
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous nature of the bet itself
jokes.explanation: Funny absurd premise - the incongruity is inherently comedic

jokes.text: Short Jewish guy against Darth Vader? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Dustin Hoffman in Star Wars premise
jokes.punchline: Physical/cultural mismatch with Darth Vader
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about physical/tonal mismatch in casting

jokes.text: Hey, Elaine, you gotta feel my pants.
jokes.setup: Kramer excited about warm clothes from dryer
jokes.punchline: Innocent request that sounds sexual
jokes.explanation: Classic double entendre - works because Kramer's innocent enthusiasm makes it funnier

jokes.text: I'll see you later. - All right. You don't know what you're missing.
jokes.setup: Elaine declining to feel Kramer's pants
jokes.punchline: Kramer's persistent innocent enthusiasm about the warmth
jokes.explanation: Follow-up that extends the double entendre naturally

jokes.text: Bold and beautiful.
jokes.setup: Describing Nicki as bold and beautiful
jokes.punchline: Reference to soap opera 'The Bold and the Beautiful'
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference without much twist - functional but not clever

jokes.text: The pita pocket prevents it from dripping.
jokes.setup: George praising his calzone eating method
jokes.punchline: Treating pita pocket like revolutionary technology
jokes.explanation: Good George moment - finding mundane innovation in simple food solution

jokes.text: If people can't hold onto things, tough luck.
jokes.setup: Discussion about lost and found
jokes.punchline: Steinbrenner's harsh dismissal of human forgetfulness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steinbrenner - ruthless businessman attitude toward basic human needs

jokes.text: Who did you bet was in Star Wars? Sammy Davis Jr.?
jokes.setup: Meeting Todd who made the Dustin Hoffman bet
jokes.punchline: Suggesting even more unlikely Star Wars casting
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation - Sammy Davis Jr. even more absurd than Dustin Hoffman

jokes.text: Two tickets. Means to an End. - Told you.
jokes.setup: Jerry doubts Nicki can get sold-out tickets
jokes.punchline: She effortlessly succeeds, proving her power
jokes.explanation: Establishes Nicki's power theme but fairly predictable payoff

jokes.text: They're really building a Utopian society up there.
jokes.setup: George describing Yankees implementing his lost and found idea
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically calling minor stadium improvement 'Utopian'
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic deflation of George's pride in minor accomplishment

jokes.text: I'm like a drug dealer. I got the guy hooked.
jokes.setup: George explaining his influence over Steinbrenner through calzones
jokes.punchline: Comparing food delivery to drug dealing
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - George's grandiose self-image applied to lunch delivery

jokes.text: So then, as I'm leaving, he gives me a look like 'Thanks for nothing.'
jokes.setup: Tip jar worker looked away during George's tip
jokes.punchline: Getting no credit for good deed due to timing
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds personal stakes to Jerry's earlier observation

jokes.text: So you don't make a habit of giving to the blind?
jokes.setup: George complaining about unacknowledged tipping
jokes.punchline: Comparing tip jar worker to blind person needing acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark turn that exposes the vanity in charitable giving - very quotable

jokes.text: Not bills.
jokes.setup: Jerry's question about giving to the blind
jokes.punchline: George's matter-of-fact admission of discrimination by denomination
jokes.explanation: Perfect George - economical admission of petty selfishness delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: She's like a beautiful Godzilla. And I'm thousands of fleeing Japanese.
jokes.setup: Describing Nicki's power to get whatever she wants
jokes.punchline: Extended monster movie metaphor with himself as victims
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor that perfectly captures power dynamic - highly quotable and visual

jokes.text: I need quarters for the dryer. - Why can't you do this on your table? - Because I don't have a table.
jokes.setup: Kramer counting change on Jerry's counter
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of not owning basic furniture
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer moment revealing his unconventional living situation matter-of-factly

jokes.text: He's found a dating loophole.
jokes.setup: Explaining Todd's betting strategy to avoid rejection
jokes.punchline: Reframing elaborate scheme as legal/technical workaround
jokes.explanation: Excellent concept - applying legal/technical language to social interaction. Very Seinfeldian.

jokes.text: That's just because you're a jackass.
jokes.setup: Jerry pointing out he never walks her home
jokes.punchline: Elaine's blunt assessment of Jerry's character
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Elaine's directness cutting through Jerry's logic

jokes.text: Crazy like a man.
jokes.setup: Elaine calling him crazy
jokes.punchline: Twisting 'crazy like a fox' to emphasize gender
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay twist on common expression but not particularly clever

jokes.text: So you and I are kind of like countrymen. Paisanos.
jokes.setup: George trying to bond with calzone shop owner
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at ethnic kinship based on surnames
jokes.explanation: Classic George cringe - inappropriate familiarity based on ethnic assumptions

jokes.text: Hey, hey, you steal my money? - No, you don't understand. I wasn't taking it out.
jokes.setup: George's tip being misinterpreted as theft
jokes.punchline: The complete reversal of his good intention
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to tip jar storyline - George's scheme backfires spectacularly

jokes.text: From 1973 to 1982, I ate the exact same lunch every day. Turkey chilli in a bowl made out of bread.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner explaining his consistency preference
jokes.punchline: Obsessive routine with bizarre food choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steinbrenner - obsessive behavior with specific absurd details

jokes.text: You eat the chilli, then you eat the bowl. Nothing more satisfying than looking down after lunch and seeing just a table.
jokes.setup: Description of bread bowl lunch
jokes.punchline: Poetic satisfaction from complete consumption/disappearance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - finding zen satisfaction in total consumption. Very quotable and absurd.

jokes.text: Was this whole bet thing just a way of asking me out? - What? - I mean, Dustin Hoffman in Star Wars?
jokes.setup: Elaine confronting Todd about the bet
jokes.punchline: Using the absurdity of the bet as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good callback that advances plot while referencing earlier absurdity

jokes.text: Must have been when I slowed down to take that curve... because for a while there, I was doing well over 100.
jokes.setup: Being caught speeding at 93 mph
jokes.punchline: Claiming he was actually going faster earlier
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation - making things worse when caught red-handed

jokes.text: Officer. Hi. Do you really have to give us a ticket? - All right, Nick. Let's hit it.
jokes.setup: Nicki trying to charm the officer
jokes.punchline: Instant success without any visible effort
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback showing Nicki's power works even on authority figures

jokes.text: I didn't have enough quarters for the dryer... but this is better and more convenient. - Oh, for both of us.
jokes.setup: Kramer using Jerry's oven to heat clothes
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic agreement about convenience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Jerry's dry response to Kramer's oblivious behavior

jokes.text: I could have got a house in Peru for 300 bucks.
jokes.setup: Discovering cigars are from Peru, not Cuba
jokes.punchline: Exaggerating how cheap everything must be in Peru
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation about economic disparity but fairly predictable

jokes.text: That's a nice name, Todd Gack. What is that, Dutch?
jokes.setup: Commenting on Todd's name
jokes.punchline: Guessing ethnicity from unusual surname
jokes.explanation: Minor observational humor about unusual name but not particularly funny

jokes.text: He fires people like it's a bodily function.
jokes.setup: George worried about Steinbrenner's reaction to no calzone
jokes.punchline: Comparing firing to involuntary biological process
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor that captures both Steinbrenner's impulsiveness and George's anxiety

jokes.text: No, I never had a job.
jokes.setup: George asking about office politics
jokes.punchline: Jerry's casual admission of complete inexperience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Jerry's detachment from normal work life

jokes.text: Kramer's cooking up some corduroy.
jokes.setup: Explaining smell from Kramer's clothes heating
jokes.punchline: Treating fabric like food ingredient
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that makes the absurd situation sound domestic and normal

jokes.text: You hang out on the west side of the building with Seinfeld all day... just laughing it up, wasting your lives.
jokes.setup: Newman explaining his dislike of George
jokes.punchline: Describing their friendship as pointless time-wasting
jokes.explanation: Good Newman moment that also functions as meta-commentary on the show itself

jokes.text: And three times a week, I shall require a cannoli.
jokes.setup: Newman making demands for helping George
jokes.punchline: Formal language for dessert extortion
jokes.explanation: Good Newman escalation - treating dessert demands with legal formality

jokes.text: I mean, unless I'm your sister, this is a date.
jokes.setup: Todd denying Saturday night with parents is a date
jokes.punchline: Using family relationship as only alternative explanation
jokes.explanation: Sharp logical argument that exposes the absurdity of Todd's denial

jokes.text: You don't work in the rain? You're a mailman. Neither rain nor sleet nor... It's the first one!
jokes.setup: Newman calling in sick for rain
jokes.punchline: George citing postal service creed with Newman's contradiction
jokes.explanation: Excellent irony - Newman violates the most famous part of postal service commitment

jokes.text: I was never that big on creeds.
jokes.setup: George pointing out postal service creed violation
jokes.punchline: Newman's casual dismissal of professional obligations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Newman - casual dismissal of duty with philosophical pretension

jokes.text: This is all burned up. Look at this. What the hell do I know about cooking a shirt?
jokes.setup: Kramer's clothes heated in pizza oven
jokes.punchline: Calzone maker's confusion about clothing as food
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to clothes heating storyline with appropriate confusion

jokes.text: You can't pay with this. - That's all I got. - Then you got no calzones.
jokes.setup: Kramer trying to pay with mountains of change
jokes.punchline: Escalating standoff over payment method
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that pays off Kramer's change obsession

jokes.text: Your clothes smell just like Paisano's.
jokes.setup: Kramer's failed calzone mission
jokes.punchline: Clothes carrying restaurant smell from oven heating
jokes.explanation: Sets up the final plot convergence but not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: That smell. I know that smell. He's in the building. Costanza is in the building. And he's not in this office.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner detecting calzone smell
jokes.punchline: Treating food smell like security breach with dramatic announcement
jokes.explanation: Excellent Steinbrenner moment - treating lunch absence like espionage with dramatic flair

jokes.text: It's like trying to smoke a chicken bone.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about the fake Cuban cigars
jokes.punchline: Comparing cigars to inedible poultry parts
jokes.explanation: Good visceral comparison that makes the cigars sound disgusting

jokes.text: What kind of a name is Todd Gack anyway? - I think it's Dutch.
jokes.setup: Jerry still puzzled by Todd's name
jokes.punchline: Kramer repeating Jerry's earlier guess
jokes.explanation: Minor callback that doesn't add much humor but provides continuity

jokes.text: We'll put all the uniforms in pizza ovens before the game. That's fantastic. Keep those muscles loosey-goosey.
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner learning about heated clothes
jokes.punchline: Immediately scaling idea up to entire team with sports terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Steinbrenner - taking random idea and immediately making it revolutionary team strategy

jokes.text: I smell a pennant!
jokes.setup: Steinbrenner excited about heated uniforms
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'smell' from clothes to predicting championship
jokes.explanation: Good closing line that uses smell motif to predict baseball success - classic Steinbrenner optimism

tmdb_overview: Jerry gets miffed when a salesman hits on Elaine while she's with him; George suspects hookers are using his car.
